about: The University of Melbourne is a public research university established in 1853, making it Australia's second-oldest university. It is a member of the Group of Eight, a coalition of Australia's leading research-intensive universities, and consistently ranks among the top universities globally. The university is renowned for its research impact and comprehensive range of study areas.
departments: Faculty of Architecture, Building and Planning
Faculty of Fine Arts and Music
Faculty of Medicine, Dentistry and Health Sciences
Faculty of Veterinary and Agricultural Sciences

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: The University of Melbourne consistently reports strong graduate employment outcomes, with a significant majority of its graduates securing full-time employment within four to six months of graduation. Median salaries are competitive, reflecting the high quality of education and the strong reputation of the university among employers. These figures are estimates based on recent Graduate Outcomes Survey (GOS) data for domestic undergraduates.

top_recruiters: ANZ (Australia and New Zealand Banking Group)
placement_highlights: The University of Melbourne consistently demonstrates strong graduate outcomes, with a high percentage of its graduates securing full-time employment shortly after completing their studies. For the most recent period (2023 data for 2022 graduates), the estimated full-time employment rate for undergraduates was around 78%, with a median salary of approximately AUD 75,000 per year. These figures are based on comprehensive graduate surveys and reflect the university's commitment to producing highly employable graduates. Salaries vary significantly by discipline, with fields like Engineering, Computer Science, and Business often commanding higher starting salaries. Graduates are highly sought after by a diverse range of industries, including major consulting firms, financial institutions, technology companies, healthcare providers, and various government departments. The university's strong industry connections, robust career services, and emphasis on practical skills contribute to these positive employment trends. While specific 'highest package' figures are not typically reported in the Australian context, the overall salary ranges are competitive, placing University of Melbourne graduates among the top earners nationally. The university's global reputation also facilitates international career opportunities for its alumni.

fees.hostel_per_year: AUD 20,000 - 35,000 (estimate, includes accommodation and some meals)

fees_note: Fees for international students are significantly higher than for domestic students. Domestic students may be eligible for Commonwealth Supported Places (CSP), where the Australian Government subsidizes a portion of the tuition fees, and students pay a 'student contribution amount'. There is no general tuition-free status for international students; all international students are required to pay full tuition fees. The fees provided are estimates for international students and can vary significantly based on the specific program of study.

scholarships.name: Melbourne International Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Up to AUD 10,000 fee remission in the first year, or 50% fee remission for the normal duration of a bachelor degree, or 100% fee remission for the normal duration of a bachelor degree.
scholarships.eligibility: International students who have applied for an undergraduate degree, achieved excellent academic results in their previous studies, and have not previously studied at a tertiary level.

scholarships.name: Melbourne Graduate Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Up to 100% fee remission and a living allowance of approximately AUD 37,000 per year (2024 rate).
scholarships.eligibility: Domestic and international students undertaking a graduate research degree (PhD or Master by Research) or a graduate coursework degree, based on academic merit.

scholarships.name: Graduate Research Scholarships
scholarships.amount: Full fee offset and a living allowance (stipend) of approximately AUD 37,000 per year (2024 rate), plus relocation grants and paid sick/parental leave.
scholarships.eligibility: Domestic and international students undertaking a graduate research degree (PhD or Master by Research), based on academic merit and research potential.

infrastructure.details: The main campus, located just north of Melbourne's CBD, featuring historic buildings, modern teaching facilities, research labs, libraries, and student services. It houses most faculties and schools.
Home to the Faculty of Fine Arts and Music, located in Melbourne's arts precinct, offering specialized facilities for music, theatre, dance, film, and visual arts.
Dedicated to veterinary science, featuring large animal hospitals, research farms, and specialized teaching facilities for veterinary students.
Focuses on horticulture and environmental studies, with extensive gardens, glasshouses, and research facilities.
Includes multiple gymnasiums, swimming pools, sports courts (tennis, basketball, squash), playing fields, and fitness studios across various campuses.
Numerous world-class research institutes such as the Bio21 Molecular Science and Biotechnology Institute, Melbourne Brain Centre, and Peter Doherty Institute for Infection and Immunity.
Provides support for academic advising, career services, health and wellbeing, disability support, and international student services.

hostel_details.total_capacity: Over 4,000 beds across various residential colleges and university-managed apartments (estimate)
hostel_details.type: Residential colleges (e.g., Ormond, Trinity, Queen's, Newman, Janet Clarke Hall, St Mary's, University College, Medley Hall), and university-managed student apartments (e.g., Little Hall, The Lofts).

transport_details.routes: The Parkville campus is well-served by Melbourne's extensive public transport network. Numerous tram routes (e.g., 19, 58) run directly past or near the campus, connecting it to the CBD, major train stations, and surrounding suburbs. Several bus routes also service the campus area, providing convenient access from various parts of the city.

library_details.journals: Access to hundreds of thousands of print and electronic journal titles (estimate)
library_details.e_resources: Millions of e-books, databases, and digital collections (estimate)
library_details.area_sqft: Extensive, spread across 12 main libraries and specialized collections, including the historic Baillieu Library and the Giblin Eunson Library.

notable_faculty.name: Professor Sharon Lewin
notable_faculty.designation: Director, Peter Doherty Institute for Infection and Immunity

notable_faculty.name: Professor Frank Caruso
notable_faculty.designation: Laureate Professor, Department of Chemical Engineering

notable_faculty.name: Professor Peter McPhee
notable_faculty.designation: Emeritus Professor, School of Historical and Philosophical Studies

faculty_achievements: University of Melbourne faculty are consistently recognised with prestigious national and international awards. This includes numerous Australian Research Council (ARC) Laureate Fellowships, election to Australia's learned academies (e.g., Australian Academy of Science, Australian Academy of Health and Medical Sciences), and significant research grants. The university's legacy also includes several Nobel laureates, reflecting a long history of groundbreaking research and academic excellence across various disciplines.
recognition: Recognized by the Australian Government as a registered higher education provider through the Tertiary Education Quality and Standards Agency (TEQSA).
additional_details: Founded in 1853, it is the second oldest university in Australia.
Consistently ranked among the top universities globally for research and teaching.
Known for its significant research output and contributions across various disciplines.
Associated with several Nobel laureates, including Sir Frank Macfarlane Burnet, Peter Doherty, and Elizabeth Blackburn.
Pioneered the 'Melbourne Model' curriculum structure, which emphasizes a broad undergraduate education followed by specialized postgraduate professional degrees.
